exercises.title: ROOT CHECK
exercises.brief: Verify an identity with strict equality.
promptLines: Define **isRoot(name)** that returns `true` only when `name` is **exactly** the string `'root'`, otherwise `false`.
Use strict equality `===` — case matters, so `'Root'` is **not** root.

tests.name: intruder -> false
tests.code: assert(isRoot('Trinity') === false && isRoot('admin') === false, 'non-root names should be false');

tests.name: case-sensitive
tests.code: assertEqual(isRoot('Root'), false, "'Root' is not 'root' — === is case-sensitive");

exercises.lore: Root access is earned, not guessed.

exercises.title: TYPE PROBE
exercises.brief: Scan a value and report its type.
promptLines: Define **probe(value)** that returns the **type** of `value` as a string, using the `typeof` operator.

exercises.title: CALLSIGN
exercises.brief: Forge a callsign from two name parts.
promptLines: Define **callsign(first, last)** that returns the **last** name and **first** name joined by a hyphen, using a template literal.
exercises.lore: On the net, your callsign is all they know.

exercises.title: POWER GAUGE
exercises.brief: Round a reading into a labelled gauge string.
promptLines: Define **powerLabel(level)** that **rounds** `level` to the nearest whole number and returns it as a labelled string.
Format: `` `Power: ${rounded}%` `` — use `Math.round` and a template literal.

exercises.title: BOOT BANNER
exercises.brief: Print a formatted login banner from variables.
promptLines: Store the operator name and node number in variables, then `console.log` a **three-line banner** built with template literals.
Declare `const user = 'Neo'` and `const node = 7`, then interpolate them with `${...}` — don't hard-code the values into the USER/NODE lines.
starterLines: // TODO: console.log the three banner lines using ${user} and ${node}

tests.name: three lines, exact
tests.code:
var lines = stdout().trim().split('\n').map(function (s) { return s.trim(); });
assertEqual(lines, ['=== THE CONSTRUCT ===', 'USER: Neo', 'NODE: 7'], 'Match all three banner lines exactly.');

exercises.hint: Three console.log calls: a literal title line, then `USER: ${user}` and `NODE: ${node}`.
exercises.lore: Welcome back, operator. The Construct is yours.
